text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town, there lived a fox named Felix. Felix loved berries. Every morning he'd sneak out of his cozy lair and chase after juicy strawberries. However, one season it rAINED cats and dogs. Felix couldn't tell berries apart anymore!

He wandered here and there, eating whatever he could find. One day he stumbled upon an old bookstore. Inside were books about everything! Felix picked up some about owls.

That night, under the moonlit sky, Felix became an owl. He felt wise. He patrolled the woods. Suddenly a rabbit darted past!

"Flee!" Felix hooted.

The rabbit froze.

"I didn't mean to hop across your sleeping grounds."

"You won't."

"What?" said Felix. He liked creatures.

"The stars!"

He thought. Owls see stars! Maybe he could see stars up close. He stretched out his neck.

Nothing moved.

Maybe it was magic?

He took a leap.

Nothing...soft
